title: conostylis micrantha
intro: Conostylis micrantha, also known as the small button flower or button wire, is a species of flowering plant in the family Haemodoraceae. This plant is native to Western Australia and has been used in traditional medicine for its various health benefits. The medicinal properties of Conostylis micrantha include its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which are attributed to the presence of compounds such as flavonoids and glycosides. Herbal preparations of Conostylis micrantha are typically made by infusing the leaves and flowers in hot water to create a tea, which can be used to treat conditions such as fever, wounds, and digestive issues.
